AutoML using pipeline templates and pipeline generator  Introduction to Automated Machine Learning

What is Automated Machine Learning (AutoML)? Automated ML refers to automating the end-to-end process of applying ML to real-world problems Advantages • Accessibility: Makes ML accessible by automating its application • Ease of Use: Allows no expert users to develop effective ML models • Efficiency: Increases ML workflow efficiency • Time saving: Reduces the time required to produce predictive models • Facilitates rapid prototyping and testing in a business context

Machine learning Vs Automated Machine Learning • AutoML is highly automated • Traditional ML requires user intervention at different stages of ML workflow • Traditional Machine Learning • Expertise Required: High level of expertise in data science • Process: Manual preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning • Time-Consuming: Each step requires multiple iterations to optimise model performance • Customisation: Allows deep customisation and fine-tuning • Barrier to Entry: High, requiring skilled analysts and complex tools

Machine learning Vs Automated Machine Learning • Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) • Expertise Required: Less, accessible to non-experts • Process: Automates data preprocessing, feature selection, hyperparameter tuning, model training and evaluation • Time-Consuming: Reduces time by automating repetitive tasks • Customisation: Allows customisation, but less than manual methods • Barrier to Entry: Lowered, making ML more accessible ➢ Streamline model development, faster and user-friendly ➢ Offers in-depth analysis and customisation at the cost of complexity and time

Why Automated Machine Learning Matters? Enhance Model Validation • Uses robust techniques like cross-validation • Ensures models generalise well to unseen data • Automates workflow, speeding up processes, improving reliability Facilitate Model Deployment • Streamlines deployment by packaging models automatically • Manages scalability and monitors performance Broaden Accessibility • Reduces the need for technical expertise • Allows non-experts to develop and deploy models • Efficient use of resources, enabling wider ML adoption  Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning Customer segmentation/personalisation • Analyse customer data to identify distinct groups • Tailor marketing strategies to each segment ➢ Increases engagement, loyalty, and conversion rates Sales Forecasting • Predict future sales trends using historical data ➢ Aids in inventory management, budget planning, decision-making Fraud Detection • Train AutoML algorithms to detect fraud patterns/fraudulent transactions ➢ Enhances security measures and reduces financial losses

Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning Supply Chain Optimisation • Forecast demand, manage inventory, Optimise delivery routes ➢ Cost savings, better customer satisfaction Predictive Maintenance • Predict equipment failures with sensor data • Reduce downtime, quality control, save costs Dynamic Pricing • Adjust prices in real-time based on supply and demand dynamics ➢ Increase revenue and market competitiveness • Streamline HR processes: analyse candidates resumes, predict turnover  Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning

Customer Support Automation • Chatbots for instant responses and efficient issue resolution Health and Safety Monitoring • Analyse health wearable data Market Dynamics • Detect patterns about market trends, data-driven decisions Content Recommendation • Enhance user experience with personalised suggestions

Deep dive into autoML using SAS Viya

What is SAS? SAS offers over 150 products within 10 solutions • Advanced Analytics, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning • Augmented Analytics & Business Intelligence • Cloud, Data Management • DataOps, Fraud & Security • Internet of Things (IoT) • Marketing Analytics • Operationalising Analytics • Risk Management

What is SAS (Statistical Analysis System)? SAS is a programming language • Used for statistical analysis • Reads data from spreadsheets and databases • Generate results in tables or graphs • Among the top programming languages for Data Scientists, alongside R and Python

What is SAS Data Mining and Machine Learning? A suite of analytics tools and software solutions offered by SAS • Extract valuable insights from data • Create predictive models, • Implement ML algorithms to solve complex problems • Enhance decision-making

Available in SAS Viya Web-based solution No software to install, it runs in the Cloud!

SAS Data Mining and ML Pipeline Pipelines are structured flows of analytics actions With various node types • Data Source Nodes Import data from various sources into SAS environment • Data Preparation Nodes Clean, transform, select key variables • Model Training Nodes • Model Assessment Nodes

Getting started with Upload Data to SAS Viya Create an ML project in SAS Viya Automate Data Exploration in SAS Viya Automate Data Transformation in SAS Viya Automate Feature Selection in SAS Viya Build ML Models in SAS Viya Evaluate and Compare ML Models in SAS Viya  Login to SAS Viya for Learners https://www.sas.com/en_au/software/viya-for-learners.html

Data Partition (training, validation, and test)

The Partition Data proportions can be edited after creating a project, but this must be done before the Data node is executed. After the Data node has been run, you can view the partition proportions, but cannot make any changes until data source is reloaded

Data Partition (training, validation, and test) • Training Set: Used to train the model • Learn patterns, relationships, and structure from this data • Validation Set: Used to tune the model • Adjust the model configuration, tuning hyperparameters • Testing Set: Used to evaluate the final model • Provide an unbiased evaluation of the model’s performance • Assess how well the model generalises to unseen data

Data Partition (training, validation, and test) Common Proportions (training-validation-test) • 70-15-15 Split: Balanced datasets for training, with sufficient data for validation and testing • 80-10-10 Split: Used for large datasets with substantial validation and testing data • 60-20-20 Split: Used when more data is required for validation and testing to avoid overfitting and ensure reliable metrics

Feature Engineering

What is Feature Engineering? Features Individual measurable properties or characteristics of a phenomenon Why Feature Engineering? Extract relevant information from raw data to make ML algorithms work effectively • Feature Selection Identify the most relevant features to use • Feature Transformation Apply mathematical transformations to features to make them more suitable for modelling • Feature Generation Generate new features from existing data • Dimensionality Reduction Reduce features number while preserving variance in the data

Why Feature Engineering?

Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/gilpress/2016/03/23/data-preparation-most-time-consuming-least-enjoyable-data-science-task-survey-says/ Select relevant features for model using criteria • AIC (Akaike Information Criterion) • BIC (Bayesian Information Criterion) • AICc (Corrected Akaike Information Criterion) • These criteria involve comparing values before and after including a variable in the model • They help decide whether a variable should be selected

Transform Data to Meet Model Assumptions • Normal Distribution: Ensure residuals follow a Gaussian distribution • Homoscedasticity: Ensure equal variance across data

Source: https://communities.sas.com/t5/SAS-Communities-Library/Changing-the-Scale-Transforming-Data/ta-p/658278

Comparing ML models

Derive Actionable Insights from Key Findings

Let's continue with the same dataset What we did • We used SAS Viya to build Linear Regression model, Neural Network model, and Decision Tree model • Discussed the Performance of the models in predicting the Target Variable 'Salary' • Compared the models in predicting 'Salary' • Discussed the best performing model 'Champion’ Next • Build refined ML predictive models using Model Hyper-Parameters Tuning • Compare and discuss the performance and output of the models before/after tuning • Generate template-based and auto-generated pipelines

Model Hyper-Parameters Tuning

What is Model Tuning? Adjusting ML algorithm's hyperparameters to improve its performance Hyperparameters of a decision tree: Splitting options • Grow Criterion used to split nodes. Common criteria include Gini impurity and entropy for classification tasks, and variance reduction for regression tasks. • Maximum number of branches In binary trees, this is typically set to 2. Higher values can control the tree's branching factor for more complex trees. • Maximum depth A deeper tree can model more complex patterns but might lead to overfitting. A smaller depth might underfit the data. • Minimum leaf size Specifies the minimum number of observations required to form a leaf. This parameter can help prevent overfitting. • Number of bins A higher number of bins allows for a more precise search for the best split but increases computational complexity.

What is Model Tuning? Adjusting ML algorithm's hyperparameters to improve its performance Hyperparameters of a decision tree: Pruning options (subtree method) • Cost Complexity Pruning (also known as Weakest Link Pruning) Trees are grown to their full size and then pruned back by removing subtrees that do not significantly improve the fit on the training data. • Reduced Error Pruning The tree is fully grown using the training set, and then nodes are pruned (or removed) if doing so reduces the error on the validation set.

Model Autotuning • What is Autotuning? • Automate the identification of the hyperparameter values that give you the most accurate model • Available for six of the Supervised Learning nodes: • Bayesian Network • Decision Tree • Forest • Gradient Boosting • Neural Network • SVM

Autotuning Search Method • Grid Search: Evaluates a model with all combinations of hyperparameter values specified in a grid, allowing you to find the best combination. • Random Search: Randomly selects combinations of hyperparameters to evaluate, offering a more stochastic approach compared to the exhaustive nature of grid search.

Parameter 2 Parameter 2

Parameter 1 Parameter 1 Grid search Random search 104  Autotuning Search Method • Bayesian Optimization: Uses the Bayesian technique for global optimization to model the performance function of hyperparameters and then chooses the next hyperparameters to evaluate by optimizing a surrogate model.

Source: Cao, Michael & Chen, Zhen & Jiang, Yi & Han, Yimo. (2022). Automatic parameter selection for electron ptychography via Bayesian optimization. Scientific Reports. 12. 12284. 10.1038/s41598-022-16041-5.  Autotuning Search Method • Genetic algorithm: Inspired by the process of natural selection and genetics, where the fittest individuals are selected for reproduction in order to produce offspring of the next generation.

Initialization Evaluation Selection Genetic Operators

Crossover Population Fitness Selection for (Recombination) Creation Calculation Reproduction + Mutation

Selected individuals are paired and combined to produce offspring. The crossover operation involves swapping parts of the genetic Each candidate solution Candidates that perform representation Generating an initial is evaluated to the best are selected to (hyperparameters) between population of candidate determine how well it take part of the two parents to create one or solutions performs reproduction process more children.

With a small probability, some parts of the offspring's genetic representation are randomly altered.

Challenges and Limitations of AutoML

Class discussion • Complexity vs Simplicity AutoML aims to simplify the model-building process by automating many steps → limits the flexibility and customization of models

• Data Quality and Preprocessing AutoML attempts to handle data quality issues, but it might not always perform as well as manual preprocessing

• Scalability and cost considerations Depending on the complexity of the models and the size of the data, scalability and performance of AutoML might become bottlenecks Running extensive AutoML processes can be computationally expensive, leading to higher operational costs.
